records.chapter: The Virtue of Moderation
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1721 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"I guarantee for the one who practices moderation that they will not face poverty."
records.arabicText: 1721 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «ضَمِنْتُ لِمَنِ اِقْتَصَدَ أَنْ لاَ يَفْتَقِرَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1721 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I guarantee for the one who practices moderation that they will not face poverty."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Providing Water
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1722 - Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"The first thing to be rewarded in the Hereafter is the charity of providing water."
records.arabicText: 1722 - قَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «أَوَّلُ مَا يُبْدَأُ بِهِ فِي اَلْآخِرَةِ صَدَقَةُ اَلْمَاءِ يَعْنِي فِي اَلْأَجْرِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1722 - Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The first thing to be rewarded in the Hereafter is the charity of providing water."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Providing Water
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1723 - Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"Indeed, Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, loves the cooling of a burning liver. Whoever quenches the thirst of a burning liver- whether of an animal or otherwise, Allah (swt) will shade them under the shade of His Throne on the Day when there is no shade except His shade."
records.arabicText: 1723 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى يُحِبُّ إِبْرَادَ اَلْكَبِدِ اَلْحَرَّى وَ مَنْ سَقَى كَبِداً حَرَّى مِنْ بَهِيمَةٍ أَوْ غَيْرِهَا أَظَلَّهُ اَللَّهُ فِي ظِلِّ عَرْشِهِ يَوْمَ لاَ ظِلَّ إِلاَّ ظِلُّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1723 - Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, loves the cooling of a burning liver. Whoever quenches the thirst of a burning liver- whether of an animal or otherwise, Allah (swt) will shade them under the shade of His Throne on the Day when there is no shade except His shade."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Providing Water
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1724 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
"Whoever gives water in a place where water is available is like one who frees a slave. And whoever gives water in a place where water is not available is like one who revives a soul. And whoever revives a soul, it is as though they have revived all of humanity."
records.arabicText: 1724 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «مَنْ سَقَى اَلْمَاءَ فِي مَوْضِعٍ يُوجَدُ فِيهِ اَلْمَاءُ كَانَ كَمَنْ أَعْتَقَ رَقَبَةً وَ مَنْ سَقَى اَلْمَاءَ فِي مَوْضِعٍ لاَ يُوجَدُ فِيهِ اَلْمَاءُ كَانَ كَمَنْ أَحْيَا نَفْساً وَ مَنْ أَحْيَا نَفْساً «فَكَأَنَّمٰا أَحْيَا اَلنّٰاسَ جَمِيعاً » »
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1724 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever gives water in a place where water is available is like one who frees a slave. And whoever gives water in a place where water is not available is like one who revives a soul. And whoever revives a soul, it is as though they have revived all of humanity."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Reward for Doing Good to the Alawiyyah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1725 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace be upon him and his family, said:
"Whoever extends a favor to any of my household, I will repay them on the Day of Judgment."
records.arabicText: 1725 - قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «مَنْ صَنَعَ إِلَى أَحَدٍ مِنْ أَهْلِ بَيْتِي يَداً كَافَيْتُهُ يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1725 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever extends a favor to any of my household, I will repay them on the Day of Judgment."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Reward for Doing Good to the Alawiyyah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1726 - Imam (as) said: "I will intercede on the Day of Judgment for four types of people, even if they come with the sins of the people of the world: a person who supported my progeny, a person who spent his wealth for my progeny in times of distress, a person who loved my progeny with his tongue and heart, and a person who worked to fulfill the needs of my progeny when they were displaced or expelled."
records.arabicText: 1726 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنِّي شَافِعٌ يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ لِأَرْبَعَةِ أَصْنَافٍ وَ لَوْ جَاءُوا بِذُنُوبِ أَهْلِ اَلدُّنْيَا رَجُلٌ نَصَرَ ذُرِّيَّتِي وَ رَجُلٌ بَذَلَ مَالَهُ لِذُرِّيَّتِي عِنْدَ اَلضِّيقِ وَ رَجُلٌ أَحَبَّ ذُرِّيَّتِي بِاللِّسَانِ وَ اَلْقَلْبِ وَ رَجُلٌ سَعَى فِي حَوَائِجِ ذُرِّيَّتِي إِذَا طُرِدُوا أَوْ شُرِّدُوا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1726 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I will intercede on the Day of Judgment for four types of people, even if they come with the sins of the people of the world: a person who supported my progeny, a person who spent his wealth for my progeny in times of distress, a person who loved my progeny with his tongue and heart, and a person who worked to fulfill the needs of my progeny when they were displaced or expelled."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Reward for Doing Good to the Alawiyyah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1727 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"When it is the Day of Judgment, a caller will announce, 'O creation, remain silent, for Muhammad wishes to address you.' Then all creation will fall silent.
The Prophet (sw), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, will stand and say: 'O assembly of creation, whoever has done a favor, shown kindness, or extended generosity to me, let them now stand so that I may repay them.'
The people will respond: 'By our fathers and mothers, what favor, kindness, or generosity do we have toward you? Indeed, the favor, kindness, and generosity are yours and Allah’s (swt) upon all creation.'
He (sw) will say to them: 'Indeed, whoever sheltered anyone from my household, treated them kindly, clothed them when they were unclothed, or fed them when they were hungry, let them stand so I may repay them.'
Then some people who have done these deeds will rise. A call will come from Allah (swt), Exalted and Glorified, saying, 'O Muhammad (sw), O My Beloved, I have made their recompense yours. Admit them into Paradise wherever you wish.'
The Prophet (sw) will then settle them in al-Waseela (a station of nearness to Allah (swt)) where they will not be veiled from Muhammad (sw) and his household (as). Blessings of Allah (swt) be upon them all."
records.arabicText: 1727 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِذَا كَانَ يَوْمُ اَلْقِيَامَةِ نَادَى مُنَادٍ أَيُّهَا اَلْخَلاَئِقُ أَنْصِتُوا فَإِنَّ مُحَمَّداً يُكَلِّمُكُمْ فَتُنْصِتُ اَلْخَلاَئِقُ فَيَقُومُ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَيَقُولُ «يَا مَعْشَرَ اَلْخَلاَئِقِ مَنْ كَانَتْ لَهُ عِنْدِي يَدٌ أَوْ مِنَّةٌ أَوْ مَعْرُوفٌ فَلْيَقُمْ حَتَّى أُكَافِيَهُ» فَيَقُولُونَ بِآبَائِنَا وَ أُمَّهَاتِنَا وَ أَيُّ يَدٍ وَ أَيُّ مِنَّةٍ وَ أَيُّ مَعْرُوفٍ لَنَا بَلِ اَلْيَدُ وَ اَلْمِنَّةُ وَ اَلْمَعْرُوفُ لِلَّهِ وَ لِرَسُولِهِ عَلَى جَمِيعِ اَلْخَلاَئِقِ فَيَقُولُ لَهُمْ «بَلَى مَنْ آوَى أَحَداً مِنْ أَهْلِ بَيْتِي أَوْ بَرَّهُمْ أَوْ كَسَاهُمْ مِنْ عُرْيٍ أَوْ أَشْبَعَ جَائِعَهُمْ فَلْيَقُمْ حَتَّى أُكَافِيَهُ» فَيَقُومُ أُنَاسٌ قَدْ فَعَلُوا ذَلِكَ فَيَأْتِي اَلنِّدَاءُ مِنْ عِنْدِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «يَا مُحَمَّدُ يَا حَبِيبِي قَدْ جَعَلْتُ مُكَافَأَتَهُمْ إِلَيْكَ فَأَسْكِنْهُمْ مِنَ اَلْجَنَّةِ حَيْثُ شِئْتَ» قَالَ فَيُسْكِنُهُمْ فِي اَلْوَسِيلَةِ حَيْثُ لاَ يُحْجَبُونَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ أَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ صَلَوَاتُ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِمْ أَجْمَعِينَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1727 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When it is the Day of Judgment, a caller will announce, 'O creation, remain silent, for Muhammad wishes to address you.' Then all creation will fall silent.
The Prophet (sw), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, will stand and say: 'O assembly of creation, whoever has done a favor, shown kindness, or extended generosity to me, let them now stand so that I may repay them.'
The people will respond: 'By our fathers and mothers, what favor, kindness, or generosity do we have toward you? Indeed, the favor, kindness, and generosity are yours and Allah’s (swt) upon all creation.'
He (sw) will say to them: 'Indeed, whoever sheltered anyone from my household, treated them kindly, clothed them when they were unclothed, or fed them when they were hungry, let them stand so I may repay them.'
Then some people who have done these deeds will rise. A call will come from Allah (swt), Exalted and Glorified, saying, 'O Muhammad (sw), O My Beloved, I have made their recompense yours. Admit them into Paradise wherever you wish.'
The Prophet (sw) will then settle them in al-Waseela (a station of nearness to Allah (swt)) where they will not be veiled from Muhammad (sw) and his household (as). Blessings of Allah (swt) be upon them all."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1728 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
"The ground of the Day of Resurrection will be fire, except for the shade provided for the believer, for his charity will shield him."
records.arabicText: 1728 - قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «أَرْضُ اَلْقِيَامَةِ نَارٌ مَا خَلاَ ظِلَّ اَلْمُؤْمِنِ فَإِنَّ صَدَقَتَهُ تُظِلُّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1728 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The ground of the Day of Resurrection will be fire, except for the shade provided for the believer, for his charity will shield him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1729 - Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"Goodness and charity eliminate poverty, increase lifespan, and protect their giver from seventy types of ill-fated deaths."
records.arabicText: 1729 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «اَلْبِرُّ وَ اَلصَّدَقَةُ يَنْفِيَانِ اَلْفَقْرَ وَ يَزِيدَانِ فِي اَلْعُمُرِ وَ يَدْفَعَانِ عَنْ صَاحِبِهِمَا سَبْعِينَ مِيتَةَ سَوْءٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1729 - Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Goodness and charity eliminate poverty, increase lifespan, and protect their giver from seventy types of ill-fated deaths."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1730 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Treat your sick ones with charity, ward off tribulations with supplication, and seek sustenance through charity. Charity releases the grip of seven hundred devils. There is nothing heavier on Satan than charity given to a believer. It reaches the hand of the Lord (azj), blessed and exalted, before it reaches the hand of the servant."
records.arabicText: 1730 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «دَاوُوا مَرْضَاكُمْ بِالصَّدَقَةِ وَ اِدْفَعُوا اَلْبَلاَءَ بِالدُّعَاءِ وَ اِسْتَنْزِلُوا اَلرِّزْقَ بِالصَّدَقَةِ فَإِنَّهَا تَفُكُّ مِنْ بَيْنِ لَحْيَيْ سَبْعِمِائَةِ شَيْطَانٍ وَ لَيْسَ شَيْءٌ أَثْقَلَ عَلَى اَلشَّيْطَانِ مِنَ اَلصَّدَقَةِ عَلَى اَلْمُؤْمِنِ وَ هِيَ تَقَعُ فِي يَدِ اَلرَّبِّ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى قَبْلَ أَنْ تَقَعَ فِي يَدِ اَلْعَبْدِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1730 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Treat your sick ones with charity, ward off tribulations with supplication, and seek sustenance through charity. Charity releases the grip of seven hundred devils. There is nothing heavier on Satan than charity given to a believer. It reaches the hand of the Lord (azj), blessed and exalted, before it reaches the hand of the servant."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1731 - "Charity given by hand protects against an evil death, wards off seventy kinds of calamities, and loosens the grip of seventy devils, all of whom urge the person not to give."
records.arabicText: 1731 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اَلصَّدَقَةُ بِالْيَدِ تَقِي مِيتَةَ اَلسَّوْءِ وَ تَدْفَعُ سَبْعِينَ نَوْعاً مِنْ أَنْوَاعِ اَلْبَلاَءِ وَ تَفُكُّ عَنْ لَحْيَيْ سَبْعِينَ شَيْطَاناً كُلُّهُمْ يَأْمُرُهُ أَنْ لاَ يَفْعَلَ».

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1732 - Imam (as) said:
"It is recommended for a sick person to give to the beggar with his own hand and to ask the beggar to pray for him."
records.arabicText: 1732 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «يُسْتَحَبُّ لِلْمَرِيضِ أَنْ يُعْطِيَ اَلسَّائِلَ بِيَدِهِ وَ يَأْمُرَ اَلسَّائِلَ أَنْ يَدْعُوَ لَهُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1732 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is recommended for a sick person to give to the beggar with his own hand and to ask the beggar to pray for him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1733 - Imam (as) said:
"Give charity early in the day, for calamities do not surpass it. Whoever gives charity at the beginning of the day, Allah (swt) will protect him from the evil that descends from the sky that day. And if he gives charity at the beginning of the night, Allah (swt) will protect him from the evil that descends from the sky during that night."
records.arabicText: 1733 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «بَاكِرُوا بِالصَّدَقَةِ فَإِنَّ اَلْبَلاَيَا لاَ تَتَخَطَّاهَا وَ مَنْ تَصَدَّقَ بِصَدَقَةٍ أَوَّلَ اَلنَّهَارِ دَفَعَ اَللَّهُ عَنْهُ شَرَّ مَا يَنْزِلُ مِنَ اَلسَّمَاءِ فِي ذَلِكَ اَلْيَوْمِ فَإِنْ تَصَدَّقَ أَوَّلَ اَللَّيْلِ دَفَعَ اَللَّهُ عَنْهُ شَرَّ مَا يَنْزِلُ مِنَ اَلسَّمَاءِ فِي تِلْكَ اَللَّيْلَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1733 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Give charity early in the day, for calamities do not surpass it. Whoever gives charity at the beginning of the day, Allah (swt) will protect him from the evil that descends from the sky that day. And if he gives charity at the beginning of the night, Allah (swt) will protect him from the evil that descends from the sky during that night."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1734 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
"Indeed, Allah (swt), there is no deity but Him, wards off through charity diseases, abscesses, burning, drowning, collapse, madness, and He (sw) peace be upon him, enumerated seventy types of evil."
records.arabicText: 1734 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «إِنَّ اَللَّهَ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ لَيَدْفَعُ بِالصَّدَقَةِ اَلدَّاءَ وَ اَلدُّبَيْلَةَ وَ اَلْحَرَقَ وَ اَلْغَرَقَ وَ اَلْهَدْمَ وَ اَلْجُنُونَ وَ عَدَّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ سَبْعِينَ بَاباً مِنَ اَلشَّرِّ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1734 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, Allah (swt), there is no deity but Him, wards off through charity diseases, abscesses, burning, drowning, collapse, madness, and He (sw) peace be upon him, enumerated seventy types of evil."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1735 - The Prophet (sw), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
"Charity given in secret extinguishes the wrath of the Lord (azj), majestic and exalted is He."
records.arabicText: 1735 - وَ قَالَ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «صَدَقَةُ اَلسِّرِّ تُطْفِئُ غَضَبَ اَلرَّبِّ جَلَّ جَلاَلُهُ ».

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1736 - Ammar narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that he said to me:
"O Ammar, charity given in secret, by Allah (swt), is better than charity given openly, and likewise, by Allah (swt), worship in secret is better than worship in public."
records.arabicText: 1736 - وَ رَوَى عَمَّارٌ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ لِي: «يَا عَمَّارُ اَلصَّدَقَةُ وَ اَللَّهِ فِي اَلسِّرِّ أَفْضَلُ مِنَ اَلصَّدَقَةِ فِي اَلْعَلاَنِيَةِ وَ كَذَلِكَ وَ اَللَّهِ اَلْعِبَادَةُ فِي اَلسِّرِّ أَفْضَلُ مِنَ اَلْعِبَادَةِ فِي اَلْعَلاَنِيَةِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1736 - Ammar narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that he said to me:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "O Ammar, charity given in secret, by Allah (swt), is better than charity given openly, and likewise, by Allah (swt), worship in secret is better than worship in public."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1737 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
"If a male beggar comes to you at night, do not turn him away."
records.arabicText: 1737 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «إِذَا طَرَقَكُمْ سَائِلٌ ذَكَرٌ بِلَيْلٍ فَلاَ تَرُدُّوهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1737 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a male beggar comes to you at night, do not turn him away."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1738 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
"Charity is rewarded tenfold, a loan eighteenfold, kindness to brothers twentyfold, and maintaining ties of kinship twenty-fourfold."
records.arabicText: 1738 - وَ قَالَ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «اَلصَّدَقَةُ بِعَشَرَةٍ وَ اَلْقَرْضُ بِثَمَانِيَةَ عَشَرَ وَ صِلَةُ اَلْإِخْوَانِ بِعِشْرِينَ وَ صِلَةُ اَلرَّحِمِ بِأَرْبَعَةٍ وَ عِشْرِينَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1738 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Charity is rewarded tenfold, a loan eighteenfold, kindness to brothers twentyfold, and maintaining ties of kinship twenty-fourfold."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1739 - Imam (as) was asked: "Which charity is the best?"
Imam (as) replied: "To a relative who harbors enmity (against you)."
records.arabicText: 1739 - وَ سُئِلَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: أَيُّ اَلصَّدَقَةِ أَفْضَلُ قَالَ «عَلَى ذِي اَلرَّحِمِ اَلْكَاشِحِ ».

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1740 - Imam (as) said: "There is no (valid) charity while a relative is in need."
records.arabicText: 1740 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لاَ صَدَقَةَ وَ ذُو رَحِمٍ مُحْتَاجٌ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1740 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no (valid) charity while a relative is in need."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1741 - Imam (as) said: "Cursed, cursed is the one who burdens others with his responsibilities. Cursed, cursed is the one who neglects those under his care."
records.arabicText: 1741 - قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَلْعُونٌ مَلْعُونٌ مَنْ أَلْقَى كَلَّهُ عَلَى اَلنَّاسِ مَلْعُونٌ مَلْعُونٌ مَنْ ضَيَّعَ مَنْ يَعُولُ ».

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1742 - Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) said:
"A man ought to provide generously for his family so that they do not wish for his death."
records.arabicText: 1742 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «يَنْبَغِي لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يُوَسِّعَ عَلَى عِيَالِهِ لِئَلاَّ يَتَمَنَّوْا مَوْتَهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1742 - Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A man ought to provide generously for his family so that they do not wish for his death."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1743 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a beggar who asks without it being known what their true condition is.
Imam (as) said: "Give to the one for whom your heart feels mercy."
Imam (as) also said: "Give him less than a dirham."
I asked, "What is the most that can be given?"
Imam (as) replied: "Four daniqs."
records.arabicText: 1743 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلسَّائِلِ يَسْأَلُ وَ لاَ يُدْرَى مَا هُوَ فَقَالَ «أَعْطِ مَنْ وَقَعَتْ فِي قَلْبِكَ اَلرَّحْمَةُ لَهُ» وَ قَالَ «أَعْطِهِ دُونَ اَلدِّرْهَمِ» قُلْتُ أَكْثَرُ مَا يُعْطَى قَالَ «أَرْبَعَةُ دَوَانِيقَ ».

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1744 - Al-Wassafi narrates from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that he said: "Among the things Allah (swt), Glorified and Exalted, confided to Musa (as) was this:
'O Musa, honor the beggar, either by giving a small amount or by responding with kindness. Indeed, those who come to you may not be humans or jinn, but angels from among the angels of the Most Merciful who test you in what I have entrusted to you. They ask you from what I have granted you, so consider carefully how you will act, O son of Imran.'"
records.arabicText: 1744 - وَ رَوَى اَلْوَصَّافِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «كَانَ فِيمَا نَاجَى اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِهِ مُوسَى عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنْ قَالَ» «يَا مُوسَى أَكْرِمِ اَلسَّائِلَ بِبَذْلٍ يَسِيرٍ أَوْ بِرَدٍّ جَمِيلٍ إِنَّهُ يَأْتِيكَ مَنْ لَيْسَ بِإِنْسٍ وَ لاَ جَانٍّ مَلاَئِكَةٌ مِنْ مَلاَئِكَةِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ يَبْلُونَكَ فِيمَا خَوَّلْتُكَ وَ يَسْأَلُونَكَ مِمَّا نَوَّلْتُكَ فَانْظُرْ كَيْفَ أَنْتَ صَانِعٌ يَا اِبْنَ عِمْرَانَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1744 - Al-Wassafi narrates from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Among the things Allah (swt), Glorified and Exalted, confided to Musa (as) was this:
'O Musa, honor the beggar, either by giving a small amount or by responding with kindness. Indeed, those who come to you may not be humans or jinn, but angels from among the angels of the Most Merciful who test you in what I have entrusted to you. They ask you from what I have granted you, so consider carefully how you will act, O son of Imran.'"

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Charity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1745 - Imam (as) said: "Give to the one who asks, even if you are on horseback."
records.arabicText: 1745 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَعْطِ اَلسَّائِلَ وَ لَوْ عَلَى ظَهْرِ فَرَسٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1745 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Give to the one who asks, even if you are on horseback."
